data.body:
## Brainstorm Outcome (scrutiny pipeline complete)

### Scope carve-outs
- P-D4 (concept drift across doc sections): Evaluated during brainstorm. Rejected as infeasible for static detection — no viable static heuristic; LLM-backed detection is out-of-architecture (this epic ships detection + blue-team routing, not LLM-backed detection engines). No follow-on ticket unless a novel approach emerges.
- P-C1 upgrade (environment-variable-gated feature flags): Moved to w21-bsnz. The P-C1 detector already lives in w21-bsnz as a bright-line pattern; the env-variable-aware variant is a direct extension of that detector, not a follow-on for this epic's scope. See companion comment on w21-bsnz.

### Success metric
Blue-team DISMISS rate <= 50% per pattern on fixture candidates. Detectors are permissive by design (cast a wide net, let blue-team discriminate); NEEDS_HUMAN is acceptable (genuine ambiguity), DISMISS is the noise signal we minimize.

### Resolver routing per pattern
- P-D1: Path A + Path B via existing delete_block/delete_file actions.
- P-T2: Path A + Path B via existing delete_function action (coverage-gap risk of deleting tests is accepted — covered in scenario analysis).
- P-C2: Path A + Path B via NEW refactor_remove_param action (extends w21-bsnz's resolver — see companion comment on w21-bsnz).

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered — no bright-line conditions fired (no new external APIs, no new dependencies, no security/auth, no novel architectural pattern, no perf optimization, no migration). Prior art already in w21-bsnz's Research Findings section.
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered (6 SCs >= 5) — 11 scenarios survived blue team filter (1 filtered as duplicate of Step 2.5 gap finding on bash-grep code-block false positives).
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none.
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered — no follow-on epics required.
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): not triggered — feasibility reviewer not dispatched (no integration signals).
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): triggered — keyword: agent definitions (adds refactor_remove_param guidance to bloat-resolver.md); graceful-degraded to conserve sub-agent usage. Behavioral risks (cascade check for parameter removal, P-C2 vs. C2 naming disambiguation) captured directly in Approach.
- Scale context (Step 0): small scale (default) — developer tool, per-repo interactive invocation.
- Fidelity review (Step 3): all dimensions >= 4 after 1 revision cycle (Value/validation_signal 3 -> 4 via SC7 addition — sprint-executable dogfooding before/after artifact).
- Cross-epic scan (Step 2.25): benign signals only — no consideration/ambiguity/conflict signals detected against 91 open epics (targeted keyword scan in lieu of full 5-batch haiku classifier dispatch).

### Session-signal override for complexity routing
- success_criteria_count = 7 (>= 7 threshold)
- scenario_survivor_count = 11 (>= 10 threshold)
- Both triggers fire — complexity override to COMPLEX, routing to /dso:preplanning (full mode).

### Brainstorm meta
- Bug filed during this session: d02a-fdc9 (brainstorm orchestrator: N independent decisions in gap-analysis step -> compressed to one composite question). Source of this finding: the orchestrator bundled P-D1/P-T2/P-C2 resolver-path decisions into a single "core question + sub-options" message, which the user correctly pushed back on.
